methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $500M - $32.0B.

data.narrative:
Multiverse Computing is a Series B–stage AI & Software company that has raised $215M+ to date and carries a current valuation exceeding $500M. The peer universe comprises 9 private companies and 1 public comparable (Palantir Technologies), all operating in the AI & Software sector at similar or later stages.

Two valuation methods triangulate the fair value range. Stage-Progression applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier to the $215M raised proxy, implying $408M—a floor reflecting capital efficiency from Series A to Series B. Peer-Median anchors to five same-sector, same-stage comparables (Safe Superintelligence at $32.0B, Physical Intelligence at $5.6B, World Labs at $5.0B, Nominal at $1.0B, and Photoroom at $500M), yielding a median of $5.0B, though the range spans $500M to $32.0B. The wide dispersion reflects heterogeneous market capitalizations within early-stage AI, from boutique software companies to frontier model developers.

The triangulated analysis supports a range of $347M (low) to $5.8B (high), with a median of $2.7B. The current valuation above $500M aligns with the lower bound. Key uncertainties include the definition of Multiverse's competitive moat within the crowded AI stack, capital efficiency relative to stage peers, and whether market valuations in this sector normalize downward. The peer-median method dominates given stage comparability, but the extreme outlier (Safe Superintelligence at $32B) signals significant dispersion in investor expectations.
